topic_summary: Stainless steel welding metallurgy: austenitic carbide precipitation and prevention (low-carbon alloys, Ti/Nb stabilization in 321/347); rapid solidification effects in laser and electron beam welding; Suutala diagram and modified Suutala diagram for cracking susceptibility; ferritic stainless steel alloys (430, 409, E-BRITE), their compositions, applications (automotive exhaust, trim), service temperature limits, embrittlement modes (sigma phase, 885°F alpha-prime embrittlement, grain growth), filler metal selection, post-weld heat treatment, hydrogen-induced cracking, and grain boundary sensitization.
speaker_attribution_flag: This recording appears to deliver Tom Eagar's 3.371 Welding Metallurgy course material but the speaker's self-reference ('taught to me originally in about a half a semester') and closing logistics ('contact information on the sheets handed out on the first day') suggest a guest lecturer or TA rather than Tom himself. Flagged for editor (Richard Taylor) review.
